cases.title: Chou Denki Card Battle: Youfu Makai (Rev 3)
cases.issue_signature: A fresh run reaches gameplay but crashes after two Left presses; the reporter found that using the in-game Initialization menu first avoided the crash, suggesting a power-on or uninitialized-memory dependency.

preconditions: Remove or archive this title's existing save and evidence files; do not select the in-game Initialization option.
Cold-launch the core and record the exact owner-computed ROM SHA-256 and every input in operator_steps.
steps: Select Scenario, then Part 1 Crimson Lotus, then From beginning.
Advance through the text to gameplay and press B to enter the playable scene.
Press Left exactly twice while recording uninterrupted video through the next ten seconds.
scenarios.expected: Gameplay remains live after both Left presses, with no crash, lockup, corrupt first table line, or core reset.

preconditions: Return to the same clean title state used for fresh-uninitialized.
Preserve the first run's capture so the paths can be compared.
steps: Select the in-game Initialization menu entry and confirm it using the exact sequence recorded in operator_steps.
Repeat Scenario, Part 1, From beginning, text advance, B, and exactly two Left presses.
Record uninterrupted video through the next ten seconds and record whether the two paths differ.
scenarios.expected: The initialized and uninitialized paths both remain live; Initialization must not be required to avoid a crash.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the same two scenarios on an original WonderSwan with the same cartridge revision; an upstream failure image or emulator run is not a correct-behavior reference.

cases.title: Meta Communication (upstream shorthand: Meta comm)
cases.issue_signature: The current MiSTer issue list reports flicker in name select and supplies no exact navigation sequence or correct-hardware capture.

preconditions: Cold-launch the owner-supplied title and record the complete navigation from boot to name select in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's cartridge revision, selected options, and name-select state.
steps: Enter name select using the recorded input sequence.
Leave the selector idle for ten seconds, then move across every available name-select position once at a steady one-input-per-second rate.
Record uninterrupted video from five seconds before entry until five seconds after the final position.
scenarios.expected: The name-select layer remains stable and matches the same-revision original-hardware capture, with no core-only flicker.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the identical name-select sequence on original hardware with the same cartridge revision because the issue report describes only the failure.

cases.title: Star Hearts: Hoshi to Daichi no Shisha Taikenban (Trial)
cases.issue_signature: The current MiSTer issue list reports flicker in the trial version's rain overlay and supplies no navigation sequence or correct-hardware capture.

preconditions: Cold-launch the owner-supplied trial build and record the exact path to a rain-overlay scene in operator_steps.
Use the same scene, dialogue state, and cartridge revision as the original-hardware reference.
steps: Navigate to the recorded rain-overlay scene without fast-forward.
Leave the scene unobstructed and unchanged for at least twenty seconds.
Record uninterrupted video including five seconds before the rain begins and the full twenty-second observation.
scenarios.expected: The rain overlay and background remain temporally stable and match original hardware, with no core-only flicker or dropped overlay frames.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the identical trial-version rain scene on original WonderSwan Color hardware; retail Star Hearts is not a substitute reference.

cases.title: Final Lap 2000
cases.issue_signature: The timing list's shorthand Final Lap report identifies a flickering track; the explicitly titled Final Lap 2000 report identifies an incorrect England GP start line. This catalogue treats both as scenarios for the one source-identified WS title rather than binding one hash to two assumed titles.

preconditions: Cold-launch owner-supplied Final Lap 2000 and record the exact race/options path in operator_steps.
Use the same course, vehicle, camera, and start state as the original-hardware reference.
steps: Start the recorded race and drive through one complete lap without fast-forward.
Record uninterrupted video from the grid through lap completion.
scenarios.expected: Track layers remain stable and match original hardware throughout the lap, with no core-only flicker.

preconditions: Cold-launch owner-supplied Final Lap 2000 and record the exact path to England GP in operator_steps.
Use the same course start state as the original-hardware reference and upstream issue image.
steps: Enter England GP and remain on or cross the start line at normal speed.
Record uninterrupted video from five seconds before the start line until ten seconds after crossing it.
scenarios.expected: The England GP start line and track match the same-revision original-hardware capture without corrupt or misplaced graphics.

cases.reference_requirement: Provide original-hardware captures for both Final Lap 2000 scenarios using the same owner cartridge revision; the MiSTer issue image is only a failure reference.

cases.title: One Piece: Grand Battle Swan Colosseum
cases.issue_signature: The report identifies garbage during a special move, a one-line glitch while changing characters at character select, and unstable health bars while jumping, but does not name characters, moves, or exact inputs.

preconditions: Record the exact path, roster positions, and transition inputs in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's mode and character-select state.
steps: At character select, alternate between the recorded adjacent characters ten times at one transition per second.
Record uninterrupted video for all ten transitions.
scenarios.expected: Every transition matches original hardware without a core-only one-line pixel glitch.

preconditions: Record selected fighters, stage, and exact jump input in operator_steps.
steps: Begin the recorded match with unchanged health.
Perform ten isolated jumps while neither fighter attacks and record the complete sequence.
scenarios.expected: Both health bars remain as stable as the original-hardware reference while jumping.

preconditions: Record fighter, opponent, stage, meter setup, and the exact special-move input in operator_steps.
steps: Trigger the same special move three times under the recorded conditions.
Record uninterrupted video beginning before input and ending after the third move completes.
scenarios.expected: All three moves match original hardware without garbage, corrupt layers, or unstable status graphics.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture all three scenarios on original hardware with the same title revision; the upstream report supplies failure descriptions only.

cases.title: Makaimura
cases.issue_signature: The map's leftmost column flashes wrong data when scrolling begins after losing a life. A comment confirms the effect in MiSTer and Mednafen but asks whether original hardware also does it; no hardware answer is present.

preconditions: Record the exact path and actions used to trigger and lose a life in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's title revision, location, and map state.
steps: Trigger gameplay and lose exactly one life using the recorded input sequence.
On the immediately following map screen, begin the same scroll as the reference.
Record loss, map entry, and the first five seconds of scrolling in high-frame-rate uninterrupted video.
scenarios.expected: The leftmost column matches the same-revision original-hardware capture frame by frame. Do not fail merely because MiSTer and Mednafen show a flash; the unresolved hardware reference decides correctness.

cases.reference_requirement: Original monochrome WonderSwan capture is mandatory because the upstream thread explicitly leaves real-hardware behavior unresolved.

cases.title: Romancing SaGa (Japan)
cases.issue_signature: The report shows sprites blinking at the lower left and lower right during text boxes but does not give the path to the pictured dialogue.

preconditions: Record the exact new-game/save path and dialogue inputs needed to reproduce the pictured text box in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's scene and dialogue page.
steps: Open the recorded text box and remain on the matched page for ten seconds.
Advance through the next ten dialogue pages at one page per second.
Record uninterrupted video including the lower-left and lower-right corners.
scenarios.expected: Corner sprites and text-box layers match original hardware without core-only blinking.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the same dialogue pages on original WonderSwan Color hardware; issue screenshots document only the reported defect.

cases.title: Digimon Battle Spirit 1.5
cases.issue_signature: The report identifies a brief corrupt frame while Digimon sprites scale into the VS screen and a graphics glitch when Up and Down are pressed rapidly while a fight is paused.

preconditions: Record mode, fighters, stage, and exact navigation to the VS screen in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's VS pairing.
steps: Enter the same VS screen five times from the same state.
Record every complete sprite scale-in at high frame rate without fast-forward.
scenarios.expected: Every scale-in matches original hardware with no missed or corrupt graphics frame.

preconditions: Record the same fight setup and pause input in operator_steps.
steps: Pause during the recorded fight.
Alternate Up and Down exactly twenty times at five presses per second, then unpause.
Record five seconds before pausing through five seconds after unpausing.
scenarios.expected: Paused and resumed graphics match original hardware without corrupt tiles, sprites, or status layers.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture both sequences on original WonderSwan Color hardware with the same revision and pairing.

cases.title: Super Robot Wars Compact for WonderSwan Color
cases.issue_signature: The mirrored MiSTer/Pocket report says black horizontal lines appear in battle; the only MiSTer follow-up asks for a core version and receives no answer.

preconditions: Record the title revision, save/new-game route, battle, units, and exact commands in operator_steps.
Match the original-hardware reference's battle state.
steps: Enter the recorded battle and execute the same attack/animation sequence three times.
Record uninterrupted video from the battle map through all three animation completions.
scenarios.expected: Battle maps and animations match original hardware without core-only black horizontal lines.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the same battle on original WonderSwan Color hardware; neither mirrored issue contains correct-output evidence.

cases.title: Engacho!
cases.issue_signature: Pocket 1.0.0 generated a nonpersistent save for EEPROM titles. The reporter's 1.0.1 beta retest says both newly created Engacho progress and cartridge-dumped saves persisted and were recognized; this catalogue does not import either private save.

preconditions: Archive any prior title save, cold-launch the owner dump, and hash the empty/initial save if one is created.
Record exact puzzle inputs in operator_steps; do not import an upstream or third-party save.
steps: Enter puzzle mode, solve puzzle 1, and quit to the title menu.
Enter puzzle mode again and confirm puzzle 2 is available.
Quit to Analogue OS, hash the generated save, relaunch the core and title, then enter puzzle mode.
Confirm puzzle 2 remains available, quit, and hash the final save.
scenarios.expected: Progress remains at puzzle 2 after the OS quit/relaunch and the save is nonempty, stable, and recognized.

preconditions: Begin from the persisted puzzle-2 state produced by new-progress-relaunch.
steps: Load a different title, return to Engacho, and confirm puzzle 2 remains available.
Power the Pocket fully off, power it on, relaunch Engacho, and confirm puzzle 2 remains available.
Hash the save after each boundary and record all values in the run log.
scenarios.expected: Puzzle-2 progress survives title switch and cold power cycle without save mutation unrelated to an in-game write.

cases.reference_requirement: Run the same sequence on the owner cartridge and original hardware; the 1.0.1 issue confirmation is a regression expectation, not a substitute for this run's reference artifact.

cases.title: Another Heaven: Memory of Those Days
cases.issue_signature: Pocket 1.0.0 lost Another Heaven's manual EEPROM save after leaving the core. The reporter's 1.0.1 beta retest says new saves persisted and the owner cartridge dump was recognized.

preconditions: Archive any prior title save, cold-launch the owner dump, and record all text-advance and save-menu inputs in operator_steps.
Do not import an upstream or third-party save.
steps: Start a new game, advance through the recorded text pages, and use the manual save function.
Return to the title menu and confirm Continue appears.
Quit to Analogue OS, hash the save, relaunch the core and title, and confirm Continue still appears.
Load Continue, advance one further page, save again, quit, and hash the updated save.
scenarios.expected: Continue remains available after relaunch, the saved scene loads, and the second valid in-game save changes the artifact deterministically.

preconditions: Begin from the valid save produced by manual-save-relaunch.
steps: Load a different title, return to Another Heaven, and confirm Continue remains available.
Power the Pocket fully off, power it on, relaunch Another Heaven, and confirm Continue remains available.
Hash the save at every boundary and record all values in the run log.
scenarios.expected: The manual save survives title switch and cold power cycle without disappearing or unrelated mutation.

cases.reference_requirement: Run the same sequence on the owner cartridge and original hardware; the closed 1.0.1 report defines a regression target but is not local reference evidence.

cases.title: Star Hearts: Hoshi to Daichi no Shisha
cases.issue_signature: The stock header declares less SRAM than the game writes, leading Pocket 1.0.0 to present a corruption prompt and wipe the save. The issue was reproduced and later closed as not actionable; a header-patched ROM would evade, not test, this compatibility case.

preconditions: Use the owner's unpatched dump with its stock header; record its SHA-256 and archive any prior save.
Record every dialogue/menu input in operator_steps. Do not apply the emulator/header patch linked from the upstream issue.
steps: Launch and select the only/default title option, then advance through character naming into gameplay.
Talk to the chief and advance his text to exit the starting tent.
Walk left two screens, enter the bottom-left hut, talk to the priestess, and receive the weapon and drum.
Exit the hut, press Y1, accept the default save choices, then hash the resulting save.
Quit to Analogue OS, relaunch the core and title, and load the save; record and hash the save before and after loading.
scenarios.expected: The stock dump starts without a corruption/wipe prompt and loads the saved state; out-of-header-range protection data is preserved across relaunch.

preconditions: Begin from the valid save produced by stock-header-save-relaunch.
steps: Load a different title, return to stock Star Hearts, and load the save.
Power the Pocket fully off, power it on, relaunch stock Star Hearts, and load the save again.
Hash the save before and after every boundary and record all identities in the run log.
scenarios.expected: The save and protection bytes survive title switch and cold boot; no corruption prompt or automatic wipe occurs.

cases.reference_requirement: Capture the same stock-cartridge path on original WonderSwan Color hardware. A patched-header ROM, emulator patch, or upstream failure capture cannot satisfy the reference.

cases.title: ws-test-suite 80186 quirks
cases.issue_signature: Open, pinned CPU sanity fixture; this is not evidence for a named commercial title.

preconditions: Verify the checked-in fixture SHA-256 before copying it to the Pocket SD card.
steps: Cold-launch the fixture and wait until the result screen stops changing.
Capture the complete screen and record whether all three source-defined result markers read PASS.
scenarios.expected: All three upstream-authored checks display PASS and remain stable.

cases.reference_requirement: The checked-in pinned open source, ROM identity, and source-defined PASS markers are the reference.

cases.title: ws-test-suite SoC interrupts
cases.issue_signature: Open, real-hardware-authored interrupt-controller sanity fixture; it is separate from commercial compatibility.

preconditions: Verify the checked-in fixture SHA-256 before copying it to the Pocket SD card.
steps: Cold-launch the fixture and wait until the result screen stops changing.
Capture the complete screen and record whether all thirteen source-defined result markers read PASS.
scenarios.expected: All thirteen upstream-authored checks display PASS and remain stable.

cases.reference_requirement: The pinned open source, exact ROM identity, and source-defined PASS markers are the reference.

cases.title: ws-test-suite internal EEPROM
cases.issue_signature: Open internal-EEPROM protocol sanity fixture; it does not exercise cartridge EEPROM persistence in Engacho or Another Heaven.

preconditions: Verify the checked-in fixture SHA-256 before copying it to the Pocket SD card.
steps: Cold-launch the fixture and wait until the result screen stops changing.
Capture the complete screen and record whether all twenty-three mono-hardware result markers read PASS.
scenarios.expected: All twenty-three source-defined mono-hardware checks display PASS and remain stable.

cases.reference_requirement: The pinned open source, exact ROM identity, and source-defined PASS markers are the reference; no cartridge-save claim follows.

cases.title: Swan Song Shift-JIS/Misaki glyph fixture
cases.issue_signature: Generated, rebuildable Japanese-text sanity fixture with licensed glyph data; it is not a proxy for any commercial game's renderer.

preconditions: Verify the checked-in fixture SHA-256 before copying it to the Pocket SD card.
steps: Cold-launch the fixture and wait for the static six-glyph display.
Capture the full screen and compare the six glyphs and their order with the fixture README.
scenarios.expected: The six licensed glyphs render in the documented order with stable black-on-white pixels and no corrupt tiles.

cases.reference_requirement: The checked-in source, pinned Misaki provenance, exact ROM identity, and documented glyph manifest are the reference.

cases.title: Wonderful medium-SRAM probe
cases.issue_signature: Generated, rebuildable Color/32-KiB-SRAM runtime sanity fixture; it is not a substitute for stock Star Hearts protection behavior.

preconditions: Verify the checked-in fixture SHA-256 before copying it to the Pocket SD card.
steps: Cold-launch the fixture and wait for its terminal screen.
Capture the complete screen and record whether MEDIUM-SRAM OK appears without a FAIL marker.
scenarios.expected: The fixture reaches and holds MEDIUM-SRAM OK with no FAIL marker.

cases.reference_requirement: The checked-in rebuildable source, exact ROM identity, and source-defined OK marker are the reference.
